Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The DECLARE model has several issues that undermine the intended insurance claim process

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The responded existence constraint for E requires A to precede it but the noncoexistence rule for E and C blocks E after C which directly conflicts with the precedence rule that forces C after R

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This creates a logical deadlock C must always exist yet E cannot coexist with C so E is effectively impossible

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The model also lacks constraints for P and N and the existence rule for C doesnt guarantee the full sequence

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Possible causes include incomplete modeling of the business rules where the noncoexistence was added to prevent premature closures but without adjusting for the evaluation step

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Another possibility is that incremental policy changes introduced the noncoexistence without updating the other rules or data quality issues led to constraints that dont match real traces
